title: Golden Crunchy Uruguayan Beef Skillet
description: A keto-friendly Uruguayan dish featuring yeasty and crunchy elements, cooked to perfection on a tava-griddle with full fat sour cream and nimono-simmered beef.
instructions: Preheat the tava-griddle to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the ground beef, yeast extract, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and black pepper, mixing well with your hands until just combined.
Form the beef mixture into 4-6 patties, depending on desired size.
Add the patties to the preheated tava-griddle and cook for 4-5 minutes per side, or until cooked to your desired level of doneness.
While the beef is cooking, mix the full fat sour cream with 1/4 cup of water to achieve a nimono-simmer consistency.
Once the beef is cooked, remove it from the tava-griddle and set aside to rest.
Add the sour cream mixture to the tava-griddle and stir to combine with the beef drippings.
Return the beef patties to the tava-griddle and spoon some of the sour cream mixture over the top of each patty.
Sprinkle the grated cheddar cheese over the top of each patty and cook until melted and bubbly.
Serve the golden crunchy Uruguayan beef skillet hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
